mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in industrial production signal expanding economic output, leading to positive earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in INDPRO coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rank information coefficient is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in both the federal funds rate and consumer price index signal a binding monetary constraint on a heated economy, jointly amplifying discount rate effects and margin pressure to generate negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not add incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression for 20-day forward returns, or the interaction strategy produces positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales predict positive S&P 500 forward returns exclusively during periods when the unemployment rate is below 4.5%, as low labor market slack amplifies the consumption-to-earnings transmission channel.
regime_hint: tight labor market with UNRATE below 4.5%
failure_condition: RSAFS increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of low-unemployment observations, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is not positive in this regime over a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 2.0% suppresses short-term discount rates and incentivizes risk-taking, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 45% of observations, or the mean forward return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production signals a broad-based expansion spanning both labor demand and goods output, producing synergistic positive earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-INDPRO interaction does not significantly predict 60-day returns (regression t-stat < 1.96) or the combined signal underperforms the equal-weighted single-factor benchmark by more than 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.5% signals sharp contraction in consumer demand, triggering earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines greater than 1.5% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of events, or the cumulative return following such triggers is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustained above 1500 thousand units indicate robust construction activity and durable goods demand, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST levels above 1500k are associated with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of observations, or the information coefficient is negative over any 2-year rolling window.
